rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Amendment To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for December 8, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiff's amendment to complaint is granted. The portion of the October 20, 2016 Amendment to Complaint to Substitute Defendants' True Names for Does 10-13, 305, and 701 at page 2, lines 18-22 is stricken. The motion is granted with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiff the opportunity to cure the defect(s) raised by the motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY GRACE VS. SOCO WEST, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for December 8, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 4. Intervenors on behalf of defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Intervenors failed to sustain their initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Rich-Tex. The evidence submitted by Intervenors, specifically plaintiff's responses to Intervenors' special interrogatories, demonstrates that plaintiff possesses, or can reasonably obtain, evidence that Rich-Tex manufactured products that contained asbestos during certain periods of time. Plaintiff's deposition excerpts that Intervenors included are insufficient to demonstrate either (1) that plaintiff did not work with/around Rich-Tex products during any point in his career or (2) that plaintiff did not work with/around Rich-Tex products during the time period when they contained asbestos. The Court considered the motion for summary judgment. Although no ground for summary judgment was identified in the Notice of Motion, the ground was made clear in the moving papers. (See Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119, 1125-1127.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
